findings.evidence: `Convergence.lean:6` has `public import TauCeti.Probability.Martingale.Crossings`; `Crossings.lean:3-5` publicly imports all helper files, whose public declarations include `AntitoneLimit.lean:44`, `AntitoneLimit.lean:289`, `Bounds.lean:38`, `Pathwise.lean:146`, `TimeReversalCrossing.lean:174`, `SigmaAlgebraHelpers.lean:37`, and `IntegrationHelpers.lean:35`.
findings.fix: Keep only `condExp_tendsto_iInf` public for this target; make the proof-chain declarations private/non-public or import the helper modules non-publicly from `Convergence.lean` instead of through a public re-export shim.
findings.issue: The shim publicly re-exports implementation files, so users of `condExp_tendsto_iInf` also get `condExp_exists_ae_limit_antitone`, `ae_limit_is_condexp_iInf`, `upcrossings_bdd_uniform`, `upBefore_le_downBefore_rev_succ`, `timeReversal_crossing_bound`, and sigma/integration helper lemmas as public API.

findings.evidence: `TimeReversalCrossing.lean:40` `@[expose] def negProcess`, `TimeReversalCrossing.lean:44` `@[expose] def revProcess`, `Crossings/Pathwise.lean:40` `@[expose] ... downcrossingsBefore`, `Crossings/Pathwise.lean:45` `@[expose] ... downcrossings`, and `Reverse.lean:54` `@[expose] ... revCEFinite`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` and keep these helpers private/non-public; if any remain public, add the normal characteristic/simp/congruence lemmas consumers need without unfolding.
findings.issue: Implementation helpers expose bodies with `@[expose]`: `negProcess`, `revProcess`, `downcrossingsBefore`, `downcrossings`, and `revCEFinite` are not the named downstream theorem and currently rely on unfoldability as their public API.

summary: The theorem surface is larger than the roadmap target needs: importing `Convergence` re-exports the whole reversal/upcrossing implementation chain. Several helper definitions also expose their bodies instead of providing a public characteristic API or staying private.
